pattern: A spiral of axis-aligned moves can only cross in three local ways, so comparing each step with the prior three or four lengths decides crossing in constant space.
hints: The path turns left each time, so a crossing is always a local interaction with a few recent segments, not a distant one.

Enumerate the small number of geometric situations: the current segment touching the one three steps back, closing a tight loop with the one four back, or spiralling outward and re-touching five back.
Compare distance[i] against distance[i-2], distance[i-3], distance[i-4] and distance[i-5] with the right inequalities for each of the three crossing shapes.

edge_inputs.description: overlap case via the four-back rule
too few moves to ever cross

stress.description: random small move lists where crossings occur frequently
stress.note: short sequences with small lengths so the brute-force segment oracle stays fast and crossings are common
